rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TITO DANILO VARGAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to Compel Enforcement of Settlement Agreement is denied. In order to take advantage of the special expedited motion procedure provided by Code of Civil Procedure §664.6 the settlement agreement must be signed by the parties themselves. It is not sufficient that the agreement is signed by a litigant's attorney of record. See Levy v. Superior Court (1995) 10 Cal.4th 578, 586; Wackeen v. Malis (2002) 97 Cal.App. 4th 429, 440. In this case, John E Boessenecker, attorney of record for W.C. Cox & Company, signed on behalf of W.C. Cox & Company. Therefore, even assuming that the Court retains jurisdiction over the putatively settled case and that the agreement is otherwise enforceable, section 664.6 is not an available mechanism to enforce it. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. on Tuesday, October 1, 2019.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TITO DANILO VARGAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To Petition To Determine Right, Title And Interest In Personal Property
rulings.Rulings: The Demurrer to Petition to Determine Right, Title, and Interest in Personal Property ("the Petition") is overruled. On demurrer, the Court cannot consider an unpleaded settlement agreement, let alone treat it as governing the parties' rights. Nor on demurrer can the Court assume that the Motion to Compel Enforcement of Settlement Agreement is successful. Moreover, even assuming that the moving party's Probate Code § 850 petition is still pending, the Petition does not involve identical parties in the same relation to each other, does not seek identical relief, and determination of moving party's petition would not necessarily completely resolve the Petition. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, October 1, 2019.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARION L. FRANCOIS LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration With Meet And Confer Requirements Of Code Of Civil Procedure Section 439 (A)
rulings.Rulings: The Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings in denied. The general rule under Probate Code § 15620 is that, absent contrary language in the trust instrument, trustees must act unanimously. Stanton v. Pries (1955) 138 Cal.App.2d 104, 106-07, however, establishes that there are certain "kind[s] of 'power'" held by trustees that are exceptions to the rule and as to which less than all trustees can act without unanimity. Although Stanton involved a trustee seeking to appeal a trial court judgment, the case suggests that the same principle would apply at the trial court level: "If, as in this case, one of the trustees believes the facts presented do not justify the exercise of such a power by the court that trustee ought in good conscience to assist the court by filing appropriate pleadings, by actively participating in the trial, and by prosecuting an appeal if he believes the trial court's decision is in error." Id. Stanton looked to Estate of Ferrall (1948) 33 Cal.2d 202, even though Ferrall did not consider the unanimity of trustee actions, as helpful in determining the kind of trustee powers that may be exercised without unanimity. The circumstance considered in Ferrall was that "the litigation does not involve merely the conflicting claims of beneficiaries to a particular fund, but concerns the performance of a duty by the trustees to protect the trust against an attack that goes to the very existence of the trust estate." Id. At 206. So here, the stakes in this litigation go to the existence of the trust because the entirety of the trust estate is at issue. Litigating this issue seems like the kind of power that each trustee individually ought to be able to litigate as part of the trustee's duty to protect the trust. Conversely, it seems anomalous under the banner of unanimity that one co-trustee ought to be able thwart the other co-trustee's effort to protect the trust corpus and effectuate the termination of the trust simply by choosing unilaterally not to give his consent to the litigation. Therefore, the Court concludes that petitioners have standing to proceed with this litigation notwithstanding one co-trustee not having joined the litigation. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, October 1, 2019.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RITA ALLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Of Executor Dale Boland For Order For Preliminary Distribution Approving Execution Of 3rd Party Pooled Special Needs Trust Sponsor Agreement And Establishing Trust Account Under Master Trust Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: a. Decedent's will names her grandchildren as the remainder beneficiaries of the pooled special needs trust, but does not identify them specifically; however, the Sponsor Agreement that was executed by Petitioner specifically names Petitioner's three children as the remainder beneficiaries. Additionally, it is unclear whether these are irrevocable gifts, or if Petitioner may change them at any time. b. The name of the sponsor in the above referenced Sponsor Agreement is stated to be Petitioner, individually, and not as the executor of this estate. c. Court to determine whether Executor should otherwise be allowed to establish the pooled special needs trust account, which is similar, but not identical to the terms of the pooled special needs trust identified by Decedent.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARL EDWARD GALLAGHER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/18/19. (No earlier availability.) No proof of service or proof of publication of the amended petition has been filed. The (corrected) proof of service filed on 8/22/19 does not indicate that the hearing is for the Amended Petition for Probate of Lost Will. No proposed order has been submitted. The caption of the amended petition is incomplete (no IAEA authority). Item 3 of the amended petition is incomplete as to the decedent's residence and citizenship. No copy of the lost will is attached to the amended petition. Nothing has been filed to overcome the presumption of revocation. Items 6 and 7 of a petition should not be completed when a decedent is survived by issue. Item 8 of the amended petition does not set forth the relationships to the decedent of the persons listed. The date of death of any heir or beneficiary must be set forth in item 8. The Court is aware that an objection has been filed.
